lines: Callum sits top of the pile on 22pts, largely because France are absolutely steamrolling Group I — they put FOUR past Norway last night, which is simultaneously brilliant for him and an absolute kick in the teeth for Al, Butters, Croft, and Wilmot, who all had Norway. Congrats on owning the same shit horse, lads.

Ellis, you magnificent fuck — New Zealand got battered 5-1 by Belgium last night, but don't worry, Argentina are playing Jordan today and Messi's lot should rack up the points you desperately need to stop being last.

The Norway massacre means Croft has taken a double hit — his Uruguay side also lost 1-0 to Spain yesterday, leaving them on 2pts and basically dead. He's haemorrhaging points like a man with no hands trying to hold water.

Carter needs a big day — Algeria vs Austria and Colombia vs DR Congo are both on today, and with three teams across those fixtures, he could claw back some ground on the leaders or continue his deeply unimpressive 14pt lurk at the bottom.

Keep your eyes on Colombia vs Portugal today — if Butters' Portugal win, they go top of Group K, but if Carter and The Foreman's Colombia stay perfect with a win, it's a two-horse race and Butters can go back to being quietly irrelevant.
